description: Senior-level biology covering classification, microbiology, botany, zoology, human anatomy and physiology, and environmental science. Provides comprehensive foundation for post-secondary biology studies.

topics.name: Unit 1: Classification & Taxonomy
topics.description: Principles of classification, taxonomic hierarchy, three-domain system, phylogenetic analysis, cladistics, dichotomous keys, and survey of kingdoms Archaebacteria, Eubacteria, Protista, Fungi, Plantae, and Animalia

topics.name: Unit 2: Microbiology: Viruses, Bacteria, & Protists
topics.description: Viral structure and replication, bacterial anatomy and metabolism, bacterial genetics, protist diversity and classification, ecological roles of microorganisms, and pathogenic microbes

topics.name: Unit 3: Fungi & Non-Vascular Plants
topics.description: Fungal structure, reproduction, and ecology; bryophyte characteristics and life cycles; seedless vascular plants; evolutionary transitions from aquatic to terrestrial environments

topics.name: Unit 4: Vascular Plant Anatomy & Physiology
topics.description: Plant cell types and tissue systems, xylem and phloem structure and function, root, stem, and leaf anatomy, transpiration and water transport, plant hormones, tropisms, and environmental responses

topics.name: Unit 5: Invertebrate Zoology
topics.description: Survey of invertebrate phyla: Porifera, Cnidaria, Platyhelminthes, Annelida, Mollusca, Nematoda, Arthropoda, Echinodermata; body plans, organ systems, and evolutionary innovations

topics.name: Unit 6: Vertebrate Zoology & Ethology
topics.description: Chordate characteristics, survey of vertebrate classes (Agnatha, Chondrichthyes, Osteichthyes, Amphibia, Reptilia, Aves, Mammalia), animal behavior, innate and learned behaviors, communication, social structures, and mating systems

topics.name: Unit 7: Human Anatomy: Support & Movement
topics.description: Skeletal system structure and function, axial and appendicular skeleton, joints and movements, muscle tissue types, skeletal muscle anatomy, sliding filament theory, neuromuscular junction, and exercise physiology

topics.name: Unit 8: Human Anatomy: Regulation & Defense
topics.description: Nervous system organization, neuron structure and function, action potentials, synaptic transmission, brain anatomy, endocrine system, hormone action, innate and adaptive immunity, vaccination, and immune disorders

topics.name: Unit 9: Human Anatomy: Maintenance
topics.description: Cardiovascular system: heart structure, cardiac cycle, blood vessels; blood composition; respiratory system structure and gas exchange; digestive system and nutrient absorption; excretory system and osmoregulation

topics.name: Unit 10: Environmental Science & Conservation
topics.description: Ecosystem components and energy flow, biogeochemical cycles, population ecology, community interactions, biodiversity and conservation, human population growth, climate change, pollution, and sustainable practices
